rationale: The citing paper uses Song and Chintagunta (2007) as a central methodological predecessor, not just background. It explicitly contrasts its own model against that paper on specific dimensions (cross-category incidence effects, homothetic versus nonhomothetic utility, and heterogeneity treatment) and states that its framework extends Song and Chintagunta's specification. The cited model is also operationalized as a benchmark competing model in fit and policy-comparison analyses, showing a clear functional role in framing and validating the contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper directly builds on Song and Chintagunta (2007)'s multicategory discrete-continuous modeling line by adding cross-category incidence effects and a more flexible utility/heterogeneity structure. It also uses the Song and Chintagunta specification as a restricted benchmark to demonstrate incremental methodological and managerial gains.
